coverage_categories.title: Primary & preventive care
coverage_categories.description: Office visits with a primary care provider, annual checkups, vaccines, and routine screenings.

coverage_categories.title: Pharmacy & prescriptions
coverage_categories.description: Generic and brand-name medications, mail-order options, and preferred pharmacy discounts.

coverage_categories.title: Mental health & counseling
coverage_categories.description: Therapy, counseling, and psychiatric services for adults, teens, and children.

coverage_categories.title: Emergency & urgent services
coverage_categories.description: Emergency room visits, urgent care, and ambulance transportation.

coverage_categories.title: Telehealth & virtual visits
coverage_categories.description: Video and phone visits with doctors, nurses, and mental health professionals.

coverage_categories.title: Lab tests & imaging
coverage_categories.description: Blood work, X-rays, MRIs, CT scans, and other diagnostic tests.

faqs.question: What if I lose my phone with my digital health card?
faqs.answer_html:
If your phone is lost or stolen, your digital health card can no longer be used from that device once you secure your account.

  1. First, sign in on another device and change your password.
  2. We recommend revoking access for the lost device in your account settings.
  3. If you need to see a doctor or pick up a prescription before you replace your phone, you can request a temporary card.

Use this link to start a temporary card request: Request a temporary card.

faqs.question: What is the digital health card and how does it work?
faqs.answer_html:
Your digital health card is a secure ID you keep on your phone instead of a plastic card. Show it at check-in, share it with your provider, or use it online to confirm your eligibility and coverage.

It displays your member ID, plan name, and important copay information. You can access it anytime by signing in to your account or adding it to your phone wallet.

faqs.question: How do I add my digital health card to my phone wallet?
faqs.answer_html:
After you sign in, go to My card and select Add to wallet. Follow the prompts for Apple Wallet or Google Wallet.

If you change phones later, simply sign in on your new device and repeat these steps.

faqs.question: How do I use my digital health card at the pharmacy?
faqs.answer_html:
At the pharmacy counter, open your digital health card on your phone. The pharmacist will scan the code or type in your member ID and BIN/PCN numbers.

For step-by-step examples, see our pharmacy usage guide in the How to use your card section.

faqs.question: What services are covered for mental health & counseling?
faqs.answer_html:
Most plans include coverage for outpatient therapy, virtual counseling, and psychiatric evaluations. Your copay or coinsurance amount depends on your specific plan.

Review the Mental health & counseling coverage summary for details about visit limits, prior authorization, and telehealth options.

faqs.question: Is eligibility for a digital health card based on my income?
faqs.answer_html:
Some discounted or zero-fee plans do use your household income and size to determine eligibility. Other plans are available to anyone regardless of income.

Use the Check eligibility tool to see which plans you qualify for based on your age, state, and income.

faqs.question: Can I use my digital health card for my child?
faqs.answer_html:
Yes. You can add children to many family plans, or you can get a child-only card if your child qualifies.

To explore options just for kids, go to Get a card for your family and select the Children (0–17) tab.

faqs.question: How will I be billed for my digital health card plan?
faqs.answer_html:
Most members pay a monthly fee with a credit/debit card or bank transfer. Your billing date is shown on the Plans & billing page when you sign in.

You can update your payment method at any time and choose to enable or disable automatic payments.

faqs.question: I can’t sign in to my account. What should I do?
faqs.answer_html:
If you see an error when signing in, first check that your email and password are correct.

  • Use the Forgot password link to reset your password.
  • Try a different browser or clear your cache.
  • If you still can’t sign in, contact support using the number on your digital health card.

faqs.question: Can I view the site in Spanish or another language?
faqs.answer_html:
Yes. Use the language selector in the top-right corner of any page and choose Español to switch the site to Spanish.

Some downloadable resources are only available in English, but we are adding more Spanish versions over time.

faqs.question: What accessibility options are available on the site?
faqs.answer_html:
We offer high contrast mode, adjustable text sizes, and keyboard-friendly navigation.

To change these settings, scroll to the footer and select Accessibility. Your preferences will be saved for your next visit on the same device.

faqs.question: How do I estimate the cost of a service like an MRI scan?
faqs.answer_html:
You can use the cost estimator to see typical prices at nearby facilities.

  1. Go to the Cost estimator page.
  2. Search for the service (for example, “MRI scan”).
  3. Enter your ZIP code and choose your distance.
  4. Select Use my digital health card benefits to see your estimated costs.

faqs.question: What happens if I see a provider who is out of network?
faqs.answer_html:
If you see an out-of-network provider, your costs may be higher and some services may not be covered.

Before your visit, use the Find a provider tool to confirm that your doctor or facility is in network.

faqs.question: How do I update my phone number or email address?
faqs.answer_html: Sign in to your account, open the Profile section, and select Contact information. You can update your phone number, email address, and mailing address there.

providers.bio: Board-certified pediatrician with 10 years of experience caring for children from birth through adolescence. Special interests include asthma management and preventive care.
Fluent in English and Spanish, Dr. Ortiz provides comprehensive pediatric care with a focus on wellness visits and school readiness.
Dr. Nguyen enjoys partnering with families to manage chronic conditions such as ADHD and allergies while supporting healthy development.
Pediatrician with a focus on adolescent health, sports physicals, and preventive screenings.
Dr. Cohen provides general pediatric care with special expertise in developmental and behavioral concerns.
Community pediatrician serving families on Atlanta’s Westside, focusing on preventive care and school health.
Family nurse practitioner providing primary care for adults and older teens, with a focus on chronic disease management.
Internist offering comprehensive adult primary care, annual physicals, and chronic disease management.
Licensed clinical social worker providing individual counseling for anxiety, depression, and stress management.
Clinical psychologist specializing in mood disorders and trauma-focused therapy for adults.
Cardiologist focusing on preventive cardiology and long-term management of heart disease.
OB/GYN providing comprehensive women’s health services, including prenatal care and annual exams.
Dermatologist specializing in medical dermatology and treatment of chronic skin conditions.
Walk-in urgent care clinic treating minor injuries, flu-like symptoms, and other same-day needs.
Physician assistant providing family medicine services for adults and children over age 5.
Multi-provider pediatric clinic offering extended evening hours and same-day sick visits.

coverage_summaries.title: Primary & preventive care coverage
coverage_summaries.summary_html:
Your digital health card helps you save on routine primary care, including annual checkups, sick visits, and preventive screenings.

  • In-network primary care visits with predictable copays
  • Annual wellness visit typically covered at low or no cost
  • Vaccines and basic screenings often covered as preventive care

Exact copays and coverage details depend on your specific plan.
coverage_summaries.visit_limits: Most plans do not limit medically necessary primary care visits, but certain preventive services may be limited to once per year.
coverage_summaries.typical_copays: Typical in-network primary care copays range from $15–$35 per visit.
coverage_summaries.preauthorization_notes: Preauthorization is rarely required for standard primary care visits, but may apply to certain procedures performed in the office.

coverage_summaries.title: Pharmacy & prescription coverage
coverage_summaries.summary_html:
Most digital health card plans include coverage for generic and brand-name medications at participating pharmacies.

  • Lowest copays for preferred generic medications
  • Higher copays or coinsurance for non-preferred brands
  • Mail-order options may offer additional savings for maintenance medications
coverage_summaries.visit_limits: Quantity limits may apply to certain medications; refills are typically allowed as prescribed within plan rules.
coverage_summaries.typical_copays: Generic medications as low as $5–$15; brand-name medications often have higher copays or coinsurance.
coverage_summaries.preauthorization_notes: Some high-cost or specialty medications require prior authorization or step therapy. Your prescriber and pharmacist will guide you through this process.

coverage_summaries.title: Summary of mental health coverage
coverage_summaries.summary_html:
Your digital health card includes coverage for a range of mental health services, including in-person therapy, virtual counseling, and psychiatric evaluations. Most plans offer the same copay for in-network therapists whether visits are in person or by video.

  • In-network licensed therapists, psychologists, and psychiatrists
  • Virtual visits via video or phone with covered providers
  • Crisis services may be covered as emergency care

Exact copays and visit limits depend on your specific plan. Check your plan details for information about deductibles, coinsurance, and eligible providers.
coverage_summaries.visit_limits: Most plans: 8–12 outpatient mental health visits per year before additional review; virtual self-guided programs are typically unlimited.
coverage_summaries.typical_copays: Typical in-network therapist copay: $20–$40 per visit for adult plans; some student plans offer $0–$15 virtual sessions.
coverage_summaries.preauthorization_notes: Preauthorization may be required for intensive outpatient programs, inpatient stays, or neuropsychological testing. Contact the number on your digital health card for details.

coverage_summaries.title: Emergency & urgent services coverage
coverage_summaries.summary_html:
Emergency services are covered when you have a serious or life-threatening condition. Your digital health card can be used at participating emergency rooms and urgent care centers.

  • Flat copay for most in-network emergency room visits
  • Lower copays for urgent care centers compared with the ER
  • Ambulance transport may be covered when medically necessary
coverage_summaries.visit_limits: No set annual visit limit for true emergencies, but frequent ER use for non-emergency issues may be reviewed.
coverage_summaries.typical_copays: Emergency room visit copays typically range from $45–$100 depending on your plan; urgent care copays are usually lower.
coverage_summaries.preauthorization_notes: Preauthorization is not required for true emergencies. Some non-emergency outpatient procedures performed in the hospital may require preauthorization.

coverage_summaries.title: Telehealth & virtual visit coverage
coverage_summaries.summary_html:
Telehealth lets you connect with doctors, nurses, and mental health professionals by video or phone. Many plans offer lower copays for virtual visits compared with in-person care.

  • 24/7 access to urgent telehealth for common conditions
  • Scheduled virtual visits with primary care and specialists
  • Secure messaging and follow-up in some plans
coverage_summaries.visit_limits: Most plans do not limit telehealth visits for common acute issues; separate limits may apply for ongoing therapy or specialty care.
coverage_summaries.typical_copays: Telehealth visit copays often range from $0–$25 depending on your plan type.
coverage_summaries.preauthorization_notes: Preauthorization requirements are generally the same as for in-person visits of the same type.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Generic allergy relief tablets (30-day supply)
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Go to a participating pharmacy and bring your paper or electronic prescription.
2. Before the pharmacist starts your order, open your digital health card on your phone.
3. Show the barcode or member ID on your card and confirm that they select the digital health card network.
4. Ask the pharmacist to fill the prescription with the lowest-cost generic option.
5. When your medication is ready, your total after the digital health card discount should be about $8.
6. Pay with your preferred payment method and keep your receipt for your records.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Ve a una farmacia participante y lleva tu receta en papel o electrnica.
2. Antes de que el farmacutico empiece tu orden, abre tu tarjeta de salud digital en tu telfono.
3. Muestra el cdigo de barras o tu nmero de miembro y confirma que usen la red de la tarjeta de salud digital.
4. Pide que surtan la receta con la opcin genrica de menor costo.
5. Cuando tu medicamento est listo, tu total despus del descuento de la tarjeta deber ser aproximadamente $8.
6. Paga con tu mtodo de pago preferido y guarda tu recibo para tus registros.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Generic blood pressure medication (30-day supply)
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Ask your doctor to send the prescription electronically to a participating pharmacy.
2. At the pharmacy counter, open your digital health card and let the staff scan the code or enter your member ID.
3. Confirm that the prescription is being filled as a generic medication.
4. The pharmacy system will apply your digital health card discounts automatically.
5. Review the price shown on the screen; for this example, your out-of-pocket cost is about $12.
6. Pay and ask the pharmacist any questions about how to take the medication.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Pdele a tu mdico que enve la receta electrnicamente a una farmacia participante.
2. En la ventanilla de la farmacia, abre tu tarjeta de salud digital y permite que el personal escanee el cdigo o ingrese tu nmero de miembro.
3. Confirma que la receta se vaya a surtir como medicamento genrico.
4. El sistema de la farmacia aplicar automticamente los descuentos de tu tarjeta de salud digital.
5. Revisa el precio en la pantalla; en este ejemplo, tu costo ser de aproximadamente $12.
6. Paga y pregunta al farmacutico cualquier duda sobre cmo tomar el medicamento.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Generic antibiotic capsules (10-day course)
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Bring your antibiotic prescription to a participating retail pharmacy.
2. Tell the pharmacist you have a digital health card and show it on your phone.
3. The pharmacist enters your member information and selects the covered generic version.
4. Before you pay, confirm that your digital health card has been applied to the prescription.
5. With the card, the example cost for this prescription is about $18.
6. Pay and review the counseling information the pharmacist provides about side effects and dosage.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Lleva tu receta de antibitico a una farmacia minorista participante.
2. Indica al farmacutico que tienes una tarjeta de salud digital y mustrala en tu telfono.
3. El farmacutico ingresa tu informacin de miembro y selecciona la versin genrica cubierta.
4. Antes de pagar, confirma que tu tarjeta de salud digital se haya aplicado a la receta.
5. Con la tarjeta, el costo de ejemplo para esta receta es de aproximadamente $18.
6. Paga y revisa la informacin que te da el farmacutico sobre efectos secundarios y dosis.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Generic insulin vials (monthly supply)
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Confirm that your pharmacy participates in the digital health card network for insulin savings.
2. Present your digital health card before the pharmacist processes the prescription.
3. Ask if there are any additional manufacturer savings that can be combined with your digital health card.
4. For this example, your discounted cost with the digital health card is about $45 for a monthly supply.
5. Schedule your next refill reminder in the pharmacy app, if available.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Confirma que tu farmacia participe en la red de la tarjeta de salud digital para ahorros en insulina.
2. Presenta tu tarjeta de salud digital antes de que el farmacutico procese la receta.
3. Pregunta si hay programas de ahorro del fabricante que se puedan combinar con tu tarjeta de salud digital.
4. En este ejemplo, tu costo con descuento con la tarjeta es de aproximadamente $45 por un suministro mensual.
5. Programa un recordatorio para tu prximo resurtido en la aplicacin de la farmacia, si est disponible.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Brand-name asthma inhaler
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Ask your doctor whether a generic or preferred alternative is available for your inhaler.
2. At the pharmacy, show your digital health card and ask the pharmacist to check coverage and copay tiers.
3. If only the brand-name inhaler is appropriate, your digital health card may lower the cost to about $60 in this example.
4. Consider using mail-order if you use this medication every month for additional savings.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Pregunta a tu mdico si existe una alternativa genrica o preferida para tu inhalador.
2. En la farmacia, muestra tu tarjeta de salud digital y pide al farmacutico que revise la cobertura y el nivel de copago.
3. Si solo el inhalador de marca es adecuado, tu tarjeta de salud digital puede reducir el costo a unos $60 en este ejemplo.
4. Si usas este medicamento cada mes, considera el servicio de farmacia por correo para ahorrar an ms.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Brand-name acne medication (topical)
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. When you drop off the prescription, show your digital health card and ask whether a covered generic is available.
2. If your dermatologist recommends staying with the brand-name medication, the digital health card can still reduce your cost.
3. In this example, your out-of-pocket amount after the digital health card discount is about $75.
4. Ask about 90-day fills if you will be using this medication long term.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Cuando entregues la receta, muestra tu tarjeta de salud digital y pregunta si hay un genrico cubierto disponible.
2. Si tu dermatlogo recomienda seguir con el medicamento de marca, la tarjeta de salud digital de todos modos puede reducir tu costo.
3. En este ejemplo, tu pago despus del descuento de la tarjeta es de aproximadamente $75.
4. Pregunta por la opcin de surtir 90 das si usars este medicamento a largo plazo.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Over-the-counter pain reliever
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Choose an over-the-counter pain reliever from the store shelf.
2. Even without a prescription, some pharmacies can apply digital health card discounts at checkout.
3. Ask the cashier to scan your digital health card from your phone before you pay.
4. For this example, your final price is about $10 after any eligible discounts.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Elige un analgsico de venta libre del estante de la tienda.
2. Aun sin receta, algunas farmacias pueden aplicar descuentos de la tarjeta de salud digital en la caja.
3. Pide al cajero que escanee tu tarjeta de salud digital desde tu telfono antes de pagar.
4. En este ejemplo, tu precio final es de aproximadamente $10 despus de cualquier descuento elegible.

pharmacy_prescription_examples.name: Over-the-counter cold and flu remedy
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_en:
1. Select an over-the-counter cold and flu product recommended by your pharmacist.
2. At checkout, open your digital health card and ask the cashier to apply any available savings.
3. In this example, your estimated cost after discounts is about $15.
4. Save your receipt so you can track how much you spend on common medicines each season.
pharmacy_prescription_examples.instructions_es:
1. Elige un medicamento de venta libre para la gripe y el resfriado recomendado por tu farmacutico.
2. En la caja, abre tu tarjeta de salud digital y pide al cajero que aplique los descuentos disponibles.
3. En este ejemplo, tu costo estimado despus de los descuentos es de aproximadamente $15.
4. Guarda tu recibo para llevar un control de lo que gastas en medicamentos comunes cada temporada.
